- Pin src/prd/rex-gateway.ts to a dedicated 'hench-gateway' zone. Currently the gateway is absorbed into the main agent zone, making the hench→rex cross-package import invisible in coupling metrics. A separate gateway zone would make the dependency measurable, auditable, and consistent with how web's gateway files (rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts) participate in cross-zone edges.
- A zone-level policy requiring all satellite zones to export their full file set through a single index.ts barrel (regardless of internal subdirectory layout) would make encapsulation measurable and enforceable — currently partial barrels (data-polling-infrastructure) are more misleading than absent ones because they imply a boundary that does not actually exist for the zone's primary consumer-facing APIs.
- Add a mandatory barrel file policy to zone creation guidelines: any zone with 2 or more consumers in different architectural tiers must provide an index.ts or named barrel that defines its stable public API surface; dashboard-panel-components (2 consumer tiers), web-dashboard (build tooling + UI consumers), and landing-page-assets (build-time consumer) all violate this policy currently
- Add a post-analysis validation step to sourcevision that flags any zone whose ID matches /-\d+$/ (numeric suffix pattern, e.g. web-2, src-2, unit-3) as requiring an explicit pin or merge decision. This mirrors the existing cross-package residual zone dissolution policy but applies it to the intra-package overflow communities that the current policy does not cover.
- Create a web-package zone health tier policy in sourcevision config that applies separate cohesion/coupling thresholds for 'service zones' (threshold: cohesion > 0.75, coupling < 0.25) vs 'composition zones' (threshold: cohesion > 0.50, coupling < 0.50) — the current single global threshold produces false positives for composition-root zones like web-dashboard and false negatives for service zones that approach the lower bound
- The orchestration tier (cli.js, ci.js, web.js) passes CLI command names, flag names, and config key paths as plain strings to child_process.spawn with no schema validation. If a domain package renames a CLI flag or config key, the orchestration script fails silently at runtime with no compile-time or startup-time signal. A lightweight constants file exporting canonical CLI flag names and config key paths, imported by both the domain package and validated in integration tests, would close this drift surface.
- Trigger a fresh sourcevision analysis on the web package and compare the resulting cross-zone import graph against the current zones.json. Multiple high-severity zone insights reference web-viewer → web-server and web-viewer → web-unit violations that appear absent from current source; if confirmed stale, flush and re-enrich to avoid compounding false findings across future enrichment passes.
- 12 zones exceed architectural risk thresholds (cohesion < 0.4, coupling > 0.6): packages-rex:tree-dag-primitives, packages-rex:schema-storage-reporting, packages-rex:task-selection-engine, packages-sourcevision:branch-collection-pipeline, packages-sourcevision:static-analysis-services, packages-rex:cli-mcp-interface, crash-recovery-system, data-polling-infrastructure, packages-rex:prd-structural-validation, packages-rex:prd-proposal-pipeline, packages-rex:integration-adapter-layer, packages-rex:llm-analysis-engine — mandatory refactoring recommended before further development
- Establish an explicit minimum cohesion threshold (e.g. 0.50) as a zone health gate for web-dashboard in sourcevision config; without a hard floor the zone will drift downward with each new mixed-concern file addition and never trigger an automated split recommendation
- Zone "Crash Recovery System" (crash-recovery-system) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.71, cohesion: 0.29, coupling: 0.71) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Add an index.ts barrel file that explicitly lists all public component exports; this gives both consumers (web-application-core and web-dashboard) a single stable import surface and makes it possible to split the zone later without auditing individual import paths across two architectural layers
- Add unit tests for throttle-controls.ts and ws-health-panel.ts — these two components have stateful or event-driven behavior (throttle configuration mutations, WebSocket status display) that warrants the same test coverage as copy-link-button.ts, and their absence creates an untested behavioral surface in the zone's most interactive components
- Move hench-runs.ts to a dedicated views zone or into web-application-core alongside other view-level page components; alternatively rename the zone to 'dashboard-components' to remove the 'panel' qualifier that falsely implies all exports are atomic leaf panels
- Extend polling/index.ts to re-export use-polling.ts and use-polling-suspension.ts alongside the current state/restart utilities; as-is the barrel covers only the polling/ subdirectory while the zone's most-imported file (use-polling.ts, 4 importing zones) is fully exposed without encapsulation.
- Zone "Data Polling Infrastructure" (data-polling-infrastructure) has critical risk (score: 0.67, cohesion: 0.33, coupling: 0.67) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Pin src/shared/glob.ts to the autonomous-agent-engine zone (or a dedicated hench-shared zone). Its current placement in hench-package-config masks a real inter-zone dependency: three autonomous-agent-engine files import it, making autonomous-agent-engine's reported coupling of 0 incorrect and hiding a refactor blast radius.
- Add an E2E smoke test (e.g. verify that index.html references the correct CSS and JS bundle paths after a build) to close the build-correctness gap — the zone's build-time coupling to landing.ts is currently unverified by any automated check
- Add 'use-project-status.test.ts' as a parallel test file to match the two-test-per-two-production-file pattern established by crash-recovery-system — the current single test file mirrors the component filename convention and leaves the hook behaviorally unverified.
- Extract the barrel/re-export declarations from task-usage.ts into a separate index.ts (public API surface only) and keep task-usage.ts as a pure implementation file — this separates the stable re-export contract from the volatile implementation detail and prevents accidental API surface changes during implementation refactors
- Add a comment block or companion manifest enumerating any CDN or external URLs referenced in index.html; without this, supply-chain security tooling cannot audit the HTML shell for outdated or compromised external dependencies invisible to pnpm audit.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @n-dx/web test --reporter=verbose' to confirm boundary-check.test.ts passes without the web-viewer → web-server violation. If it passes, trigger a fresh sourcevision analysis to flush the stale cross-zone metrics — the existing zone insights cite these 4 imports as a critical enforcement gap that may no longer exist.
- cli.js, ci.js, web.js, and config.js are plain JS with no type checking. Adding JSDoc @type annotations tied to package-exported config schemas and enabling tsc --checkJs would surface schema-drift bugs at the orchestration layer without requiring a full TypeScript migration.

- crash-detector.ts is placed under performance/ but serves crash recovery, not performance monitoring — the directory name creates a false affordance; relocating to a crash/ subdirectory and adding an index.ts barrel would align location with intent.
- No internal barrel file exists to formalize the hook→component API surface; adding a status/index.ts would allow the hook and component to evolve independently without callers needing to track both module paths.

- Without a barrel, crash-detector.ts is directly importable from outside the zone, bypassing any future encapsulation boundary; this is a lower-severity but growing risk as the zone is referenced by 3 external import paths.
- hench-runs.ts is a full view/page that composes panels, yet it lives inside the panel component zone — this inverts the expected composition hierarchy (pages compose panels) and means the zone contains both leaf components and a composite consumer of those components
- web-viewer imports polling 8 times while polling imports web-viewer only 3 times — the dependency is asymmetric and directional (web-viewer depends on polling). The 3 reverse imports from polling into web-viewer should be audited to confirm they are Preact lifecycle calls (useState/useEffect) rather than reaching into unrelated viewer concerns.
- parse-gateway-exports.mjs audits the same gateway rules enforced by tests/e2e/domain-isolation.test.js, but the two are completely decoupled — the E2E test suite never invokes the script, so the script can silently diverge from the enforcement logic without any CI signal.
- Build-time coupling between landing-page-assets (HTML/CSS) and web-dashboard (landing.ts entry point) is opaque to import-graph analysis — zone health metrics show 0 coupling but the zones share a feature boundary; this silent coupling is a maintenance risk if the landing page build pipeline changes
- domain-isolation.test.js enforces gateway rules at the file-import level, but parse-gateway-exports.mjs performs the same analysis outside the test pipeline; if the two analyses ever disagree, there is no automated way to detect the discrepancy — consider driving one from the other.
- Gateway files (rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts, external.ts) are embedded inside the largest zone (268 files) rather than in a dedicated integration layer — if web-application-core is later split, these gateways will need to be extracted first to avoid breaking the single-gateway-per-upstream-package invariant
- landing.ts entry point in web-dashboard and the HTML/CSS files in landing-page-assets form a split-ownership pattern for a single feature — neither zone fully owns the landing page, creating a weak zone boundary around this feature
- boundary-check.test.ts enforces the web-internal layering rule (web-server above web-viewer) but the cross-zone import data shows web-viewer → web-server: 4 imports — an upward dependency that this test should be catching; verify whether those 4 imports are type-only (erased at runtime) or genuine runtime violations.

- Perfect cohesion (1.0) on a 28-directory, 149-file zone is a health-monitoring blind spot: mutual imports across all subsystems lock the cohesion score at 1.0 permanently, preventing automated split recommendations even as semantically unrelated concerns accumulate. Define and enforce a manual decomposition policy (e.g., >100 files OR >15 directories triggers a sub-zone audit) rather than relying on the cohesion metric alone.
- crash-detector.ts is directly importable from any file in the web package with no barrel enforcing single-entry-point access; future callers can bypass use-crash-recovery.ts and consume the detector directly, causing recovery state to diverge from detection state without any type-system or lint guard.
- The zone has two consumers at different architectural depths (web-application-core: 5 imports, web-dashboard: 1 import) but no barrel file defining a stable public API; any internal restructuring propagates to import sites in both zones simultaneously with no contract boundary to absorb the change
- hench-runs.ts is a full page/view that composes panel components, yet it lives in the dashboard-panel-components zone alongside the leaf components it consumes; this creates an intra-zone hierarchy inversion where a page-level file resides in a component-level zone, conflating two different abstraction levels and making it impossible to measure the zone's cohesion accurately without knowing which files are leaves versus composites
- polling-restart.ts has no unit test coverage; restart logic involves stateful transitions that integration and E2E tests are unlikely to exercise exhaustively, leaving a behavioral gap in the polling subsystem.
- use-polling.ts functions as a package-wide hub (4 importing zones) but is assigned to a satellite zone with cohesion 0.33; hub files in satellite zones misrepresent the zone's stability profile and make its coupling metric unreliable — the file's connectivity warrants placement in web-viewer or a dedicated polling-primitives layer.
- parse-gateway-exports.mjs audits gateway exports outside the test pipeline with no automated cross-check against domain-isolation.test.js verdicts; integrate the script into CI and diff its output against test assertions to surface drift at merge time rather than during manual audits.
- domain-isolation.test.js and parse-gateway-exports.mjs enforce overlapping gateway invariants via independent code paths with no shared source of truth; a gateway pattern update that modifies one without the other will pass CI undetected indefinitely.
- use-project-status.ts hook likely has zero dedicated test coverage: the zone's only test file matches the component name, not the hook name, creating an untested stateful module in a zone that already has the minimum viable file count.
- No barrel file exists for the zone, so web-viewer must maintain 3+ direct import paths to individual page module files; any internal rename or split requires coordinated multi-site updates in web-viewer with no single entry point to update.
- Three page components that render sourcevision JSON output have zero test coverage and no schema contract; a breaking change to sourcevision's output format will corrupt all three views silently, with no unit, integration, or snapshot test to detect the regression.
- usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts imports loadPRDSync from prd-io.ts (line 37) directly rather than receiving it through the same dependency-injection channel used for collectAllIds; this undocumented direct coupling partially undermines the DI inversion and means the zone has a second, uncontrolled dependency on web-application-core internals that will not be caught by the existing DI pattern documentation
- web-application-core's 0.92 cohesion metric aggregates both server-layer and viewer-layer files into a single score, hiding the bidirectional imports between these layers (web-viewer→web-server: 4, web-server→web-viewer: 1) that would be flagged as upward-dependency violations under CLAUDE.md's web-internal zone stack if the zone were correctly split
- web-dashboard reaches directly into the polling infrastructure zone (3 imports) bypassing web-application-core as the integration hub; in the stated hub-and-spoke model all cross-zone dependencies from satellite zones should route through web-application-core, making these 3 lateral edges undocumented topology violations
- boundary-check.test.ts flags the web-viewer → web-server import question as an open concern but does not assert against it; the test responsible for enforcing web-internal layering has an unverified gap for the single highest-risk cross-zone edge in the package.
- God function: cmdAnalyze in packages/sourcevision/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts calls 32 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions

- Create a packages/hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts (alongside rex-gateway.ts) to concentrate all @n-dx/llm-client imports into a single file. This mirrors the existing rex-gateway.ts pattern and completes the gateway discipline for both upstream dependencies hench has. Without it, llm-client imports are unconstrained and may drift across the 159-file zone as agent capabilities expand.
- Add an explicit assertion to architecture-policy.test.js that enforces zero inbound imports into any file in packages/rex/src/ and packages/sourcevision/src/ from the @n-dx/web package. The current domain-isolation.test.js guards gateway imports but does not exhaustively guard against web-to-domain import direction inversions. The two confirmed violations (sourcevision→web-server, rex-unit→web-server) passed CI, indicating the current test does not cover these paths.
- Both domain packages lack a tests/integration/ directory tier. Establish a shared convention (e.g., a TESTING.md in the monorepo root) that mandates an integration test tier for each package and specifies what in-process contract scenarios it must cover — particularly gateway re-exports and store mutation correctness — before the integration gap widens further.
- CLAUDE.md prohibits backwards-compatibility re-export shims, but packages/web/src/viewer/performance/crash-detector.ts is exactly such a shim — created when crash-detector moved to crash/ and never cleaned up. Audit all re-export-only files across the codebase for the same pattern: any file whose entire content is re-exports pointing to a new canonical location is a shim candidate for deletion after updating its importers.
- Create packages/web/tests/unit/shared/ and relocate tests for web-shared utilities (node-culler.test.ts) from tests/unit/viewer/ into it. Enforce the convention in boundary-check.test.ts: a test file whose primary production import target is classified in zone X must reside under a directory corresponding to X, not a sibling zone's directory.
- Establish a documented admission criterion for the cross-package integration tier: any new gateway module (rex-gateway, domain-gateway, external.ts) must have a corresponding integration test added to tests/integration/contracts/ within the same PR that introduces the gateway. This prevents the current situation where 8-function gateways exist with zero integration-tier coverage.
- Introduce a cohesion CI gate at threshold 0.5: the current bimodal distribution (all healthy zones ≥0.95, all problem zones ≤0.44) means this threshold would catch all current structural outliers (fix, web, route, web-shared) without flagging any currently healthy zone, making it a zero-false-positive enforcement rule.
- The '"./dist/*": "./dist/*"' wildcard subpath export present in all package.json files (as documented in CLAUDE.md's conventions table) creates a package-wide bypass of the public.ts API contract. Adding a lint rule or CI check that flags direct dist/ imports in consumer files (e.g., import from 'rex/dist/internal/foo') would convert this undocumented escape hatch from an implicit footgun into an explicit, tracked exception.
- The CLAUDE.md gateway table entry for hench lists '8 functions (store, tree, task selection)' — verify hench/src/prd/rex-gateway.ts export count matches this number. If it has grown beyond 8 without a documentation update, the 'explicit, auditable surface' guarantee for the gateway pattern is broken and the table should be updated to reflect the current count.
- The cross-zone import map shows 'rex-unit → web-server' and 'sourcevision → web-server' as the two domain-tier violations, but neither 'rex-unit' nor 'sourcevision' matches the canonical zone IDs 'rex-prd-management-core' and 'sourcevision-analysis-engine' — zone alias resolution in the import map may be silently masking which actual zone owns the violating file, making targeted remediation unreliable. Audit the alias mapping to confirm which files are responsible.
- The e2e test file cli-dev.test.js is a single point of failure for dev-mode coverage analogous to scheduler-startup.test.js for server boot. Both files should be annotated in CI configuration as required tests (not skippable) to prevent silent coverage gaps when refactoring dev-server startup paths.
- The intra-domain isolation rule ('rex and sourcevision never import each other') is stated in CLAUDE.md but has no corresponding test assertion. Add a single test in domain-isolation.test.js that scans packages/rex/src/ for any import matching 'sourcevision' and packages/sourcevision/src/ for any import matching 'rex|@n-dx/rex'. This closes the one remaining enforcement gap in the four-tier hierarchy: horizontal domain-tier isolation is currently trust-based rather than test-enforced.
- The three single-edge cycle-completing imports (web-server → web-viewer, web-viewer → route, web-viewer → web-unit) have persisted across multiple analysis passes with no remediation. Create discrete tracked tasks for each edge with the specific file and symbol to relocate — open-ended observations about bidirectional cycles do not drive resolution; named tasks with specific targets do.
- The viewer-message-pipeline zone (src/viewer/messaging/, 7 files) is prescribed as an independent architectural tier in CLAUDE.md but has no corresponding detected zone and no independent health metrics. Add archetype annotations to messaging files so zone tooling can surface their coupling independently from the broader web-application-core zone.
- Add subdirectory-level barrel files (analyze/index.ts, store/index.ts, schema/index.ts) that explicitly whitelist the symbols each sub-directory exposes internally; this converts the currently convention-based intra-zone layering into a mechanically enforceable boundary that would have prevented the reverse import from the fix zone.
- Split the sourcevision test tiers into a separate 'sourcevision-tests' zone analogous to rex-cli-e2e-tests — bundling all production source and all test tiers into one 144-file zone prevents per-tier cohesion metrics from being computed and hides test coverage distribution from zone-level health tooling.
- Cohesion of 0.96 across 355 files is statistically insensitive: any individual file change moves the metric by ≤0.003. Do not rely on cohesion thresholds in CI to detect early structural regressions in this zone — supplement with direct import-graph edge count assertions on the specific boundary files (external.ts, rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts) instead.
- The viewer-message-pipeline internal layer (src/viewer/messaging/, 7 files) is subsumed into this zone with no independent health metrics. Assigning archetype annotations to the messaging files would allow zone tooling to surface its coupling independently without requiring a full re-analysis.
- Zone name 'peripheral' contradicts quantitative coupling (0.6): a zone with 14 bidirectional imports to core is not peripheral by any structural measure. Rename to reflect its actual role (e.g., 'viewer-supplemental-components') to avoid misguiding contributors who use the name as a priority signal during triage.
- Audit crash zone's 3 imports from web-viewer to determine if the internal cohesion (1.0) is achieved by coupling all four files to the same web-viewer type or hook. If so, the zone's high cohesion score is misleading — it reflects shared viewer-layer coupling rather than independent crash-recovery design. The goal should be cohesion around crash semantics, not cohesion around a shared viewer dependency.
- Zone name 'crash-recovery-system' implies ownership of both crash detection and recovery, but the production recovery hook (use-crash-recovery.ts) lives in packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ — outside this zone. The zone actually owns only a re-export shim and tests. Rename the zone to reflect its actual scope, or consolidate the canonical crash/crash-detector.ts and use-crash-recovery.ts into a single dedicated crash/ zone separate from web-viewer.
- packages/web/src/viewer/performance/crash-detector.ts is a re-export shim pointing to ../crash/crash-detector.ts and violates CLAUDE.md's explicit prohibition on backwards-compat re-export shims. Identify all importers of the performance/ path and update them to import from src/viewer/crash/ directly, then delete the shim. The shim is also the reason the zone analysis misidentifies this zone's canonical production file.
- Add an integration test for the hench→rex-gateway pipeline: instantiate a PRDStore, have the gateway select a task, and verify status update propagates correctly. This is the highest-value missing contract test given hench's rex-gateway.ts imports 8 functions from rex.
- The fix zone has cohesion 0.25 AND bidirectional coupling with rex-unit — both the fragile-zone risk indicators simultaneously. This combination (low cohesion + bidirectional coupling) means changes to either zone require understanding both, with no stable interface between them. This is the highest-risk intra-domain structural combination in the codebase and warrants immediate remediation over web-package cycles, which are better documented.
- The 2-file integration tier should grow proportionally with the number of cross-zone import edges, not the number of packages. With 15 cross-zone edges currently tracked, a minimum of 5-7 integration test files (one per architectural boundary: web-server↔web-viewer, viewer↔web-shared, hench↔rex-gateway, web↔domain, etc.) would provide boundary-specific failure messages rather than a single omnibus failure.
- The './dist/*' wildcard subpath export in package.json creates a bypass path around public.ts for all packages including llm-client. For the foundation tier, this is particularly risky: hench or a future consumer could import llm-client internals directly, then have those imports break silently when internal files are renamed. Consider restricting the llm-client package.json to only the '.' export pointing to public.ts and removing the dist wildcard, or documenting the wildcard as an intentional escape hatch with a stability disclaimer.
- Add a test in tests/e2e/architecture-policy.test.js that parses each root orchestration script (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) for static import/require statements and fails if any are found. The spawn-only boundary is the most important architectural rule in the codebase but is currently enforced only by convention — making it machine-checkable would catch accidental violations before they reach main.
- Update integration-coverage-policy.test.js to enforce a minimum ratio (e.g., integration tests >= 15% of e2e test count) rather than a static floor, so the policy stays proportional as the suite grows.
- Add a cli-mcp.test.ts to this zone that validates the 'rex mcp' command startup and basic tool invocation through the stdio transport; the current nine test files cover all CLI commands except MCP, leaving an untested entry point in the rex command surface.
- Create a dedicated tests/integration/ directory within the rex package with at least one test covering the store→tree→task-selection pipeline in-process; the current direct jump from unit to E2E means store mutation regressions require a full CLI spawn to detect.
- A 4-file zone with coupling 0.6 means the majority of its import edges are external — this zone has more cross-zone connections than internal ones. Zones with coupling > cohesion are structural liabilities: they import more than they encapsulate. Either absorb route into web-shared (for primitives) or web-viewer (for components) based on which host better reflects each file's actual purpose.
- register-scheduler.ts and shared-types.ts lack unit test counterparts despite the zone establishing an implicit 1:1 pairing convention. Add tests/unit/server/register-scheduler.test.ts and tests/unit/server/shared-types.test.ts (or a shared types validation test) to restore consistent coverage.
- viewer-route-state is the only zone with both cohesion (0.4) and coupling (0.6) simultaneously at warning thresholds — the fragile zone anti-pattern. As a merger candidate: move route-state.ts (pure routing logic) into web-shared and use-route-state.ts (Preact hook) into web-viewer proper. This eliminates the zone, resolves both metrics, and removes the bidirectional cycle with web-viewer in a single redistribution.
- The 'web' zone (28 files, cohesion 0.40) participates in a confirmed 14+14 bidirectional cycle with web-viewer, has low internal cohesion, and carries a misleading name. This combination of three independent risk indicators (low cohesion, confirmed cycle, naming collision) makes it the highest-priority zone rename and structural audit target outside the direct-violation fixes.
- Introduce an explicit admission criterion for web-shared: 'a file belongs in web-shared only if it (1) has zero framework imports (no Preact, no Express), (2) is consumed by at least two distinct layers above it, and (3) exposes a cohesive abstraction'. Without this criterion, web-shared functions as a residual zone — the place files go when they don't fit anywhere else — which explains both its low cohesion and its role as a target for cycle-breaking relocations.
- Create a src/viewer/api.ts (or src/viewer/index.ts) barrel that explicitly re-exports the symbols consumed by crash, route, web-unit, and web zones. This single file would serve as the inbound API contract for web-viewer, making its external-facing surface visible, auditable, and decoupled from internal module organization. The existence of external.ts for outbound imports but no equivalent for inbound imports is an asymmetry in the current gateway discipline.

#### Address relationship issues (11 findings)

```rex-meta
id: "510283d6-0316-4555-a175-582e4d7f3541"
level: task

priority: critical

resolutionType: code-change
source: sourcevision
startedAt: "2026-03-11T01:33:59.267Z"
_passthrough:

      - c3c09d9b374e

      - 0dd901285f35
      - db75a187fabc
      - 0c9cc06433a8
      - b46ac02dd933
      - c9140f2f0077
    category: relationship
    severityDistribution:
      warning: 7
      critical: 4
    findingCount: 11
```

- crash-recovery-system imports 3 symbols from web-viewer, creating a dependency on the layer it guards. If those imports are Preact hooks or component utilities, a renderer-level failure could silently break the recovery path. Prefer importing only framework-agnostic primitives (e.g., from web-shared) within crash recovery logic to keep the safety net independent of the failing layer.
- With only 2 files guarding the cross-package contract surface while 33 web-viewer→web-server imports exist at runtime, the integration tier is significantly under-invested relative to the coupling it is supposed to guard — each heavy cross-zone edge should have a corresponding contract test here.
- The landing page feature is structurally split: static assets (HTML/CSS) here, landing.ts in web-dashboard-peripheral-views. Consolidating landing.ts into this zone (or into a dedicated 'landing' directory) would give the marketing surface a single, self-contained home.
- rex-prd-management-core imports 1 symbol from the web-server zone, creating an upward dependency from the Domain tier into the web composition root. Domain packages must not import from the web layer — the shared symbol should be relocated to the Foundation tier or a neutral shared module.
- sourcevision-analysis-engine imports 1 symbol from the web-server zone — a Domain-tier package importing upward into the web composition root violates the four-tier hierarchy and the gateway-ownership rule that makes domain-gateway.ts the exclusive consumer of sourcevision APIs.
- rex-unit → web-server (1 import) and sourcevision → web-server (1 import) both terminate in this zone; if either import targets an analytics service file rather than the gateway files, it bypasses the enforced gateway boundary. Auditing the exact import targets is warranted.
- The single back-edge from web-viewer into viewer-route-state inverts the expected dependency direction: route state should be a pure input to the viewer, not a consumer of it. If the reverse import is a runtime value (not a type-only import), this constitutes a soft cycle that could cause initialization ordering issues in the Preact component tree.
- web-server → web-viewer: 1 import closes the cycle with the 33-import reverse flow, producing a full bidirectional dependency between the two heaviest internal sub-zones. Even a single runtime import from server into viewer defeats the 'viewer is built separately and served as static assets' contract.
- web-viewer → web-server: 33 imports violate the stated internal layering contract (CLAUDE.md: web-server is composition root, viewer must not import upward into server). If any of these are runtime imports they represent a critical layer inversion.
- web-viewer ↔ web-unit bidirectional (7+1 imports) creates a secondary internal cycle; the single return import from web-viewer into web-unit should be traced to confirm it is a type-only import flowing through external.ts rather than a runtime dependency.
- landing.ts is co-located in this zone (by import graph) while the static landing assets (HTML/CSS) form a separate landing-page-static-assets zone; the feature is split across two zones with no shared import backbone, making the landing surface harder to maintain.

- Bidirectional coupling: "rex-fix-command" ↔ "rex-prd-engine" (7+1 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
- Bidirectional coupling: "web-application-core" ↔ "web-viewer-messaging-pipeline" (22+16 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
- Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects
- High coupling (0.71) — 11 imports target "web-application-core"
- Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Cohesion of 0.29 is below the 0.4 threshold, likely because the community-detection algorithm sees the four files as loosely connected within the broader viewer graph; semantically they are tightly related around hench observability.
- Coupling of 0.71 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold; the zone has no internal edges compared to its 11 outbound imports into web-viewer, making it a pure leaf consumer cluster — splitting it further would not reduce coupling.
- High coupling (0.75) — 7 imports target "rex-prd-engine"
- Low cohesion (0.25) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Coupling 0.75 reflects a thin command layer that delegates heavily to rex-unit — acceptable for a CLI shim, but the reverse edge (rex-unit → fix: 1 import) creates a bidirectional dependency that should be audited for circular risk.
- Rex dogfoods its own PRD tooling by storing .rex/ state inside the package directory; this is architecturally intentional but the execution-log.jsonl committed to the repo should be gitignored to avoid noisy diffs.
- 20 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- web-unit imports web-application-core 7 times; since routes-data.ts is a server route handler, confirm these imports are type-only (stripped at compile time) and do not introduce a runtime dependency from server routing logic into viewer modules.
- sourcevision (domain layer) imports this web-server zone once; domain packages should not depend on web-tier modules — verify this reference is a type-only import from shared-types.ts that can be relocated to a foundation-tier package if it grows.
- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports

- Add a monorepo-wide invariant test asserting that every zone with cohesion below 0.4 is either a data zone (no source files), a test fixture zone, or has an explicit waiver comment in architecture-policy.test.js. Zones currently below threshold without waivers: fix (0.25), panel (0.29). The test would prevent low-cohesion zones from silently accumulating as new files are added.
- CLAUDE.md documents the web package's internal zone layering as a linear stack (web-server → web-viewer → messaging → web-shared), but the actual import graph forms a hub topology with web-viewer at the center (11 distinct cross-zone edges). The linear model cannot be topologically sorted given current imports. Update CLAUDE.md to document the hub topology and web-viewer's role as the shared-state hub, so contributors do not introduce new imports based on an incorrect mental model of the dependency direction.
- Change the REQUIRED_CONTRACTS coverage check in integration-coverage-policy.test.js from package-directory-name matching to gateway-file-path or gateway-exported-symbol matching so each entry in the list is independently verified; the current strategy silently conflates multiple gateways sharing the same package name (hench rex-gateway + llm-gateway both match on 'hench')
- Establish a policy that every critical or warning finding produced by sourcevision zone enrichment must be accompanied by a corresponding policy-test assertion within the same PR that resolves the finding. The current workflow (analyze → insight → eventual fix) allows findings to age indefinitely; a 'findings must be enforced or explicitly waived' policy would create a mechanical link between detection and resolution.
- The three pending one-line fixes (crash-detector.ts ViewId import path, hench-runs.ts barrel migration, ActiveRun added to barrel) are individually small but together resolve the hench-agent-monitor and viewer-crash-recovery zone violations flagged in previous analysis passes. Batching them into a single 'barrel and import path hygiene' PR would complete the web package's outstanding structural fixes with minimal risk.
- The three web package zones with simultaneously low cohesion AND participation in bidirectional cycles — web (0.55), crash (0.5), panel (0.29) — are the monorepo's structural risk concentration. All three are governed only by zone conventions (enforcement tier 3). Adding a boundary-check.test.ts assertion for each zone's import direction would promote them to enforcement tier 2 and yield the highest risk reduction per unit of effort of any single architectural improvement available.
- The src/analyze/ directory contains 8+ specialized services with no documented test-pairing rule. Add a CI assertion (or README mapping table) that requires a tests/unit/analyze/<service>.test.ts counterpart for every src/analyze/<service>.ts file, preventing coverage gaps from accumulating silently in the zone's largest subdirectory.
- This is the only web zone meeting the joint fragility criterion (cohesion < 0.6 AND coupling > 0.3). Its 10 entry points expose a wide API surface into bidirectionally-coupled neighbors. Treat refactoring changes in this zone as high-risk until cohesion improves or the catch-all content is redistributed.
- Add a boundary assertion in boundary-check.test.ts specifically for crash-detector.ts: assert it does not import from viewer/external.ts and does import ViewId from shared/view-id.ts. This converts the existing code comment contract into a machine-checked invariant, preventing the misrouted import from silently re-appearing after refactoring.
- The REQUIRED_CONTRACTS gateway coverage check in integration-coverage-policy.test.js matches on package directory name ('hench') rather than gateway path, so one reference to 'hench' in cross-package-contracts.test.js satisfies both the rex-gateway and llm-gateway entries; change the hasReference check to require the gateway file path or a gateway-specific symbol name to make each contract independently verifiable
- Audit whether the single reverse import (rex-unit → fix, 1 edge) is a type-only import or a runtime import. If runtime, it creates the Domain tier's only bidirectional cycle and should be resolved by moving the shared symbol to a neutral location within the rex package (e.g., src/shared/).
- Add ActiveRun (and ActiveTasksPanelProps if consumed externally) to components/index.ts so the barrel can serve as the exclusive entry point for all hench-agent-monitor exports. Without it, hench-runs.ts cannot fully migrate to barrel imports even if it wanted to.
- Update hench-runs.ts lines 19-24 to import all six panels from '../components/index.js' rather than from individual component files — the barrel already exports all five value components, and adding ActiveRun would remove the last blocker for full barrel adoption.
- The REQUIRED_CONTRACTS weak-match means the llm-gateway contract (packages/hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts, 30 functions + 10 types) is not independently verified at integration level; add a dedicated assertion block in cross-package-contracts.test.js that imports and exercises at least one symbol from the llm-gateway to close the gap the policy test currently fails to detect
- rex-fix-command is the only rex zone with BOTH low cohesion (0.25) AND high coupling (0.75) — the canonical fragile-zone signature. Its 2 production files carry no independent domain weight, meaning any interface change in rex-prd-engine forces a corresponding change here. Inlining fix.ts into rex-prd-engine's CLI command layer eliminates the fragile satellite without loss of functionality.
- .rex/execution-log.jsonl is tracked by git and modified on every hench run, generating permanent diff noise in code review. Add it to .gitignore to prevent operational state from contaminating source history and reduce accidental staging risk.
- Rename or re-classify the 'rex-unit' zone to 'rex-core' or 'rex-prd-engine' to disambiguate it from the test zones that share the '-unit' suffix convention. The current name is the only production zone in the monorepo that uses a suffix reserved for test zones.
- boundary-check.test.ts should add an assertion that crash/crash-detector.ts does not import from viewer/external.ts — the one-line import change needed has been identified but has no automated guard preventing it from being re-introduced or missed in review.
- Introduce a src/index.ts or src/public.ts entry point for the web seam zone that explicitly declares which symbols are part of its stable interface, analogous to the src/public.ts convention used by every npm package. The absence of a declared API surface is a structural reason why the web↔web-viewer bidirectional coupling has 38 edges rather than a focused set.
- Add a boundary-check assertion that files importing hench-agent-monitor panel components (active-tasks-panel, concurrency-panel, memory-panel, ws-health-panel, throttle-controls) must do so via components/index.ts rather than direct file paths — this enforces the barrel contract that already exists but is currently bypassed by hench-runs.ts.
- Add a boundary-check.test.ts assertion that no file in src/shared/ imports from src/viewer/, src/server/, or src/viewer/messaging/. This is the missing symmetrical enforcement for the foundation layer: web-shared's zero-coupling property is documented and observed but not mechanically protected.
- The fix for crash-detector.ts importing ViewId is one line: change '../external.js' to '../shared/view-id.js' (or '../shared/index.js'). Until this is done, ViewId's placement in web-shared provides no practical isolation benefit because the import still traverses the viewer layer's external.ts gateway.
- Add a boundary-check.test.ts assertion that viewer-layer files do not import directly from server-layer modules — enforce that all server-originated types entering the viewer flow through src/viewer/external.ts. This converts the CLAUDE.md gateway documentation into a machine-verified invariant, analogous to how domain-isolation.test.js enforces the package-level gateway pattern.

- hench-agent-monitor bypasses the web-shared foundation layer entirely (0 imports) and depends exclusively on web-viewer (11 imports) — any future need for shared utilities will route through the viewer abstraction rather than the designated foundation layer
- The fix command zone has net zero structural contribution beyond delegation — with 7 imports from rex-prd-engine and only 1 reverse edge, it is closer to a pass-through adapter than a domain zone; if its only role is translating CLI args to rex-prd-engine calls, it could be inlined into the CLI command layer without architectural loss.
- crash ↔ web-viewer (3+2 crossings) and panel ↔ web-viewer (11+4 crossings) both create bidirectional satellite relationships with the same hub zone; the pattern of multiple low-cohesion satellite zones bidirectionally coupled to one high-cohesion core zone is a hub-and-spoke anti-pattern that makes web-viewer changes high-risk.
- packages/web/src/viewer/crash/crash-detector.ts imports ViewId from external.ts (viewer zone) while packages/web/src/viewer/performance/index.ts imports from crash/index.js — bidirectional dependency between the 'crash' detected zone and 'web-application-core' forms a cycle; crash should depend on viewer types through a one-way edge only.
- web-viewer imports 1 symbol from web-unit (a test zone) — production code acquiring a runtime dependency on a test zone is a structural boundary violation that could accidentally pull test-only utilities into the production bundle; identify the import and relocate the shared symbol to web-shared or a dedicated viewer utility module.
- routes-data.ts (server route handler, 3-file zone) imports 7 symbols from the 338-file web-application-core zone which contains viewer components; the asymmetric edge count (7 in from the small zone, 1 out from the large zone) indicates routes-data.ts is consuming viewer-side abstractions — these must be verified as type-only imports to prevent server code from acquiring a Preact runtime dependency.
- sourcevision (domain tier) has 1 import targeting the web-server zone that likely resolves to web-task-usage-scheduler/shared-types.ts; this inverts the four-tier dependency hierarchy at a concrete file level — the referenced type must be relocated to a foundation-tier package (@n-dx/llm-client or a new shared package) to restore tier integrity.
- routes-mcp.ts has exactly one consumer (server/start.ts) inside the server composition root but landed in the messaging-pipeline zone; this placement splits the MCP request lifecycle across two zones and obscures the full server routing surface during audits.

- Bidirectional coupling: "web-peripheral" ↔ "web-viewer" (15+21 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface

- Coupling of 0.75 with only 4 files suggests the fix command is functionally part of rex-core but was split off by community detection — consider this a zone-map artifact rather than an intentional boundary.
- The bidirectional zone-level import cycle (fix ↔ rex-unit) warrants verification that src/core/fix.ts does not import from src/cli/commands/fix.ts, which would violate the CLI/core layering rule.
- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Bidirectional imports with web-viewer (21 outbound, 15 inbound) indicate mutual coupling; resolving the cycle would make both zones more independently testable and reduce the peripheral zone's coupling score of 0.45.
- Build configuration files (build.js, dev.js, package.json) co-exist with runtime source files (landing.ts, viewer components), blurring the boundary between build infrastructure and application code.
- Cohesion of 0.55 is below the healthy threshold; grouping files by purpose (build tooling vs. runtime UI periphery) into separate zones would improve discoverability and zone metrics.
- 14 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- One reverse import from web-server back into web-viewer exists; verify it is not a runtime import that breaks the server/viewer build boundary, as the viewer is built separately and served as static assets.
- Bidirectional imports between crash and web-viewer indicate a loose cycle: crash imports from web-viewer and web-viewer imports from crash. Per CLAUDE.md, crash is a sub-zone that should receive imports from the hub, not contribute back to it — consider whether crash's outbound imports to web-viewer can be inverted via a callback or event interface.
- Cohesion of 0.46 is just below the healthy threshold; the mix of constant files (data-files, view-id) and a stateful utility (node-culler) may be worth splitting if the zone grows.

- A vitest config-level include pattern that restricts tests/integration/ to **/*.test.js (rejecting .ts files) would enforce the dist/-only import guarantee automatically and catch extension mistakes in CI rather than silently degrading the zone's fidelity contract.
- Intra-package gateway modules (web external.ts and api.ts) are not validated for the re-export only no-logic invariant that domain-isolation.test.js enforces for cross-package gateways. Extending the gateway logic check to cover intra-package gateways would close a systematic enforcement gap and make the policy symmetric across all gateway types.
- No CI gate enforces a minimum cohesion threshold for source zones. Zones below 0.5 cohesion (fix: 0.25, web-shared: 0.46, crash: 0.5) are flagged only in analysis reports. Adding a zone health gate to ci.js (minimum cohesion 0.5 for source zones with 5 or more files) would prevent silent accumulation of incoherent utility zones over time.
- The server and viewer independently define the FeatureToggle API contract shape (routes-features.ts and views/feature-toggles.ts). Audit all other API response types defined in server route files to identify additional silent duplications in viewer code — the FeatureToggle case is likely not unique. A one-time grep for interface/type definitions in routes-*.ts whose fields also appear in viewer/views/*.ts would surface the full duplication set.
- The web package has a shared-types.ts file in the server zone whose viewer-consumed types are the probable source of the 32 web-viewer→web-server import edges. A systematic audit of shared-types.ts exports — classifying each as server-only, viewer-only, or framework-agnostic — would produce a concrete relocation plan: server-only types stay, viewer-only types move to viewer/types.ts, and framework-agnostic types move to web-shared. This single audit would likely resolve the 32-edge anomaly that has been flagged across multiple prior enrichment passes.
- web-shared satisfies both fragility criteria (cohesion: 0.46, coupling: 0.54) while serving as the three-consumer foundation of the web package. Establish a formal addition policy for web-shared: a new module should have at least two distinct consumer zones before being added to web-shared; single-consumer utilities belong closer to their dominant use site. Document this in CLAUDE.md under the web-shared zone description.
- Zone-confirm that packages/web/src/schema/v1.ts is in web-viewer (not web-server). If confirmed, the 32 web-viewer→web-server edges are genuine server-zone type imports by viewer components, not schema imports. This makes relocating the source of those 32 imports (likely types.ts and shared-types.ts) to web-shared the highest-leverage single action to resolve the 32-edge coupling anomaly.
- Add a top-of-directory README.md or a comment block in each test file explicitly documenting the .js-extension convention and the reason (prevent TypeScript alias resolution, preserve dist/-only boundary). Without this, the next contributor adding an integration test will default to .ts and silently disable the zone's core guarantee.
- The web peripheral zone combines cohesion 0.55 with bidirectional imports from both web-viewer (15 edges) and web-server (2 edges) — high coupling AND low cohesion simultaneously. Audit whether the 15 web-viewer imports can be reduced by extracting shared types to web-shared; this would raise cohesion and lower coupling in a single pass.
- FeatureToggle and FeaturesResponse are duplicated between routes-features.ts (server) and views/feature-toggles.ts (viewer). The viewer definition was written from scratch rather than sharing the server's type — a concrete manifestation of the missing server/viewer contract layer. Extract both interfaces to src/schema/v1.ts or a new src/schema/features.ts and import from there in both files. This resolves a silent drift risk: a server field change currently requires a manual viewer update with no compile-time failure.
- packages/web/src/server/shared-types.ts is a probable root cause of the 32 web-viewer→web-server edges: if viewer components import server-side shared types from this file, those imports register as viewer→server boundary crossings. Moving framework-agnostic types from shared-types.ts into web-shared would allow viewer to import them without crossing the server zone boundary and would reduce the 32-edge count to zero for those specific imports.
- external.ts is missing a re-export for SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES from shared/data-files.ts. shared/index.ts exports all three constants (DATA_FILES, ALL_DATA_FILES, SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES) but external.ts only surfaces the first two. Add SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES to external.ts to close the gap and prevent future viewer code from bypassing the gateway with a direct shared/ import to access supplementary file names.
- web-shared has cohesion 0.46 AND coupling 0.54 — the only production zone meeting both the low-cohesion and moderate-coupling thresholds simultaneously. This combination makes it fragile: changes have wide blast radius while the zone lacks the internal coherence to absorb or predict those changes. Treat web-shared as a risk zone requiring active governance on additions.

- crash ↔ web-viewer (2+2 symmetric cycle): equal edge counts in both directions are a strong signal of an unextracted shared interface. Define a crash-boundary type module in crash/ or web-shared to break the cycle.
- data-routes imports 7 symbols from web-viewer. Route handlers should produce data consumed by the viewer, not depend on viewer-internal types. Verify all 7 imports flow through viewer/api.ts; any that reach into viewer-internal modules represent a layering inversion between the server route and UI layers.
- rex-core exports to rex-fix-command (7 imports) while also importing from it (1 import), creating a bidirectional dependency where the canonical core depends on a satellite command — the reverse edge must be removed to restore layering
- High coupling (0.75) is entirely attributable to 7 imports from fix into rex-core, which is correct; the 1 reverse import from rex-core into fix is the sole violation and the only edge that needs removal
- sourcevision-engine has 1 outbound import into web-server, which is a tier-inversion: Domain importing from Web Execution violates the four-tier hierarchy and compromises package independence
- usage-tracking-scheduler has 1 outbound import into web-viewer. Server-side scheduling code importing from the viewer layer (which is compiled separately and served as static assets) is a build-boundary violation if the import is runtime rather than type-only. Trace and confirm it is an `import type` or relocate the shared symbol to web-shared or web-server.
- web-landing (HTML/CSS) and landing.ts (web-peripheral) form an implicit subsystem split across two zones with zero shared import edges. Because HTML and CSS are invisible to the import graph, this split is undetectable by all graph-based tooling and must be resolved by physical co-location.
- web-peripheral imports directly from web-server (2 edges), bypassing the web-viewer hub. CLAUDE.md documents hub-and-spoke with web-viewer at center; a direct peripheral→server edge is architecturally undocumented and adds a hidden composition-root dependency to an already heterogeneous zone.
- web-viewer has 1 import into web-unit (a test zone). Production code depending on test infrastructure is a critical boundary violation — the dependency must be inverted or the shared symbol relocated to a production module.
- web-viewer imports from web-unit (1 edge), meaning production hub code depends on test infrastructure — this edge should be eliminated or the shared symbol moved to a production module
- web-viewer imports from web-unit (1 edge): production code must never import from a test zone. Trace the import and relocate the shared symbol to a production module or web-shared.
- web-viewer → web-unit (1 import) inverts the production/test dependency direction: production hub code imports from a test-only zone. This is the sole instance in the codebase where production source depends on test infrastructure and must be eliminated — move any shared type or utility needed by both into web-shared or a dedicated types file inside web-viewer.

- Add a non-importable file extension exclusion list to sourcevision zone detection (PNG, JPG, SVG, GIF, HTML, CSS, MD, JSON, YAML, lock files). Binary assets and markup files cannot form import edges, so including them in zone detection produces noise zones (logo, web-landing) with false-positive 'at-risk' labels and distorted cohesion scores of 0. This single change would eliminate at least 3 noise zones and remove misleading risk labels from the zone map.
- Add subdirectory grouping to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ to make the three-zone split visible in the file tree. Create commands/chunked-review/ and commands/fix/ subdirectories for the satellite zone files, leaving the remaining 27+ core-zone files in the flat commands/ root. This mirrors the convention used elsewhere in the monorepo and gives the community detection algorithm a directory signal to produce stable zone assignments.
- CLAUDE.md's web-shared governance section states it is 'the only production zone meeting both fragility thresholds simultaneously' — this is factually incorrect. rex-cli (0.25/0.75) and fix (0.25/0.75) also meet both thresholds. Update the documentation to list all three dual-fragility zones and apply consistent governance to each.
- Establish a monorepo-wide zone governance policy that applies the dual-threshold fragility test (cohesion < 0.5 AND coupling > 0.5) uniformly across all packages — not just web-shared. Both rex satellite zones currently breach both thresholds but have no governance response. A single governance rule in CLAUDE.md covering all packages prevents the current situation where identical metric signatures receive different treatment depending on which package they appear in.
- The implicit web-package load order (web-shared → viewer-message-pipeline → web-dashboard-platform → web-server) has no mechanical enforcement. Add a zone-cycle assertion in boundary-check.test.ts or architecture-policy.test.js that validates no new import cycle is introduced across these four zones — the existing zone-cycle detection covers package-level cycles but not intra-web-package load-order violations, leaving the web package's internal layering unguarded.
- boundary-check.test.ts uses regex-based import path extraction that is blind to dynamic imports, making it structurally unable to enforce policies against the dynamic import patterns already identified in rex (analyze.ts) and any future dynamic imports in web. Supplement boundary-check.test.ts with a separate dynamic-import-audit test (or a grep-based CI check) that enumerates all 'await import()' call sites across package source directories and asserts that none cross zone boundaries without documentation.
- hench-agent (160 files, 31 directories) has no documented intra-zone governance despite being the second-largest zone in the monorepo. Establish a governance document for hench parallel to CLAUDE.md's web-shared section: identify sub-zone boundaries (agent/, prd/, brief/), document index.ts barrel requirements, and add boundary assertions to hench's test suite before the zone reaches web-viewer's scale.
- Add a ZONE_BOUNDARY marker comment or README to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ explaining which files belong to the satellite zones (chunked-review, prd-fix-command) vs the core zone. A flat directory with three zone memberships and no sub-directories creates invisible boundaries that only sourcevision can detect.
- The production→test import from web-dashboard-platform into web-unit combined with 7 reverse imports from web-unit back into web-viewer forms a closed production↔test cycle, not a simple one-directional violation. Treat remediation as requiring both edges to be resolved simultaneously — eliminating only the production→test direction will leave a unidirectional cycle that still couples test zone stability to production API changes.
- Establish a rex-satellite governance policy in CLAUDE.md analogous to the web-shared policy: both satellite zones (chunked-review, prd-fix-command) have cohesion 0.25 and coupling 0.75, meeting the same dual-threshold condition that triggered web-shared governance. Without a policy, these zones will continue accepting new files with no fragility guardrail.
- crash (cohesion 0.5, bidirectional web-viewer coupling) is one poorly-placed addition away from dual-fragility. Apply the two-consumer rule proactively to new crash zone additions before cohesion degrades further.
- fix has cohesion 0.25 and coupling 0.75 — both exceed fragility thresholds, yet CLAUDE.md's governance section does not list it as a dual-fragility zone. Apply the same active governance policy applied to web-shared (addition review, consumer count checks) to fix immediately.
- Relocate packages/rex/src/core/fix.ts to rex-prd-engine by auditing whether its imports are exclusively to other rex-prd-engine files. If confirmed, the file was misassigned to prd-fix-command by import-proximity clustering. Moving it removes the zone's only non-CLI file and makes the zone name accurate.
- rex-cli has cohesion 0.25 and coupling 0.75 — both exceed fragility thresholds, matching the profile CLAUDE.md reserves exclusively for web-shared. Update CLAUDE.md to acknowledge rex-cli as a dual-fragility zone and apply equivalent active governance.
- shared-types.ts (permanent, cycle-breaking, wide blast radius) and the scheduler services (transient, event-driven, narrow blast radius) have incompatible stability contracts but share a zone. Consider moving shared-types.ts to src/shared/ to correctly reflect its zone-neutral role and decouple its governance from the transient service lifecycle — a change to shared-types.ts should trigger broader review than a change to register-scheduler.ts, but the current zone co-location makes them indistinguishable.
- The 'web' zone's bidirectional coupling with web-viewer (16 in + 6 out) combined with its 2 imports from web-server creates a hidden secondary coupling path web-server→web→web-viewer that inflates the real viewer-server dependency beyond the direct 37-edge count. Audit the 6 web-viewer→web edges and 2 web→web-server edges to determine whether they represent the same type contracts as the direct viewer→server edges, making the secondary path redundant and removable.
- The web↔web-viewer cycle (2.7:1 ratio) is the most tightly mutually dependent cycle in the monorepo. Unlike lopsided cycles resolvable by elevating a few reverse imports through a shared interface, this cycle likely requires a structural decision: fully absorb 'web' into web-viewer, split its responsibilities into web-server, or formalize it as an explicit composition shell with a documented API contract.
- boundary-check.test.ts uses regex-based import extraction, which is blind to dynamic imports (await import()), template-literal paths, and multiline import statements. Any new enforcement assertion (including the proposed two-consumer rule check) will inherit these blind spots. Document this limitation in a comment at the top of boundary-check.test.ts and consider whether the two critical dynamic import paths in analyze.ts and cli/index.ts need a separate enforcement mechanism.
- web-landing's summary.json reports fileCount: 2 but the files array contains 3 entries (index.html, landing.css, landing.ts) — a data integrity mismatch. Verify the zone serialization path handles HTML/CSS file counting correctly and does not silently drop non-TypeScript files from the count.
- shared/index.ts is the only cohesion mechanism in this zone — it is a barrel exporting 3 constants and 1 type from two semantically unrelated files. No automated check enforces that consumers import through index.ts rather than directly from data-files.ts or view-id.ts. If direct imports proliferate, zone cohesion becomes unmeasurable and the two-consumer rule cannot be applied per-module. boundary-check.test.ts should assert that web-shared consumers import through the barrel index rather than from leaf files directly.

- Add a build-file exclusion list to sourcevision configuration: packages/web/build.js, packages/web/dev.js, and any future *.config.js or build-tooling files should be excluded from zone membership analysis. These files share directory proximity with runtime components but have no runtime import relationships with them — their inclusion corrupts cohesion and coupling metrics for any zone they land in.
- Add a hooks/index.ts barrel to packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ and a boundary-check.test.ts assertion requiring consumers to import through the barrel. The hooks directory is the largest leaf-import surface in the web viewer (29 files) with no enforcement — analogous to the pre-barrel state of crash/ and shared/ before their boundary assertions were added.
- CLAUDE.md describes crash as having 'bidirectional coupling with web-viewer' but current imports show only web-viewer → crash (viewer imports crash's recovery utilities) with crash importing web-shared directly (documented bypass) rather than web-viewer — update the governance table to accurately describe the actual dependency direction to prevent engineers from applying the wrong mitigation strategy.
- Consolidate the 'Required Tests' table into TESTING.md as the single source of truth. Replace the corresponding CLAUDE.md table entry with a reference link to TESTING.md. Add an architecture-policy test assertion that scans required test file paths for the 'REQUIRED TEST' annotation string, making the required-test contract machine-verifiable in CI rather than documentation-only.
- Create a monorepo-level 'injection seam registry' alongside the gateway module table in CLAUDE.md. Seams that use callback injection (start.ts → register-scheduler.ts) rather than gateway imports are invisible to static analysis — listing them explicitly with their injected interface types prevents future contributors from inadvertently replacing injection with direct imports to 'simplify' the code.
- Extend build-output-contract.test.ts to cover server bundle artifacts (dist/server/ or equivalent). The current contract is browser-only (viewer + landing), leaving server build regressions undetected in CI until a deployment failure.
- Neither the rex package nor the hench package has an intra-package one-way dependency direction test despite having 4 active bidirectional cycles between them (rex: 3, hench: 1) — adding a single assertion per package that no file in src/ imports from a higher-tier directory unless it is itself in that tier would provide the same CI regression protection that web's boundary-check.test.ts provides for the web package.
- The current zone analysis data contains at least one confirmed phantom edge (web-viewer → web-server: 39) that has been driving architectural governance discussions for multiple analysis passes. Before the next governance pass, re-run sourcevision analysis to produce a fresh zone map. All architectural priorities derived from stale cross-zone edge counts should be treated as provisional until confirmed by fresh analysis.
- The llm-foundation './dist/*' wildcard export and the absence of sourcevision entry point declarations together mean any file in the foundation tier can be imported directly by any intra-monorepo consumer without triggering a lint, type, or boundary test warning. The foundation tier is the most critical boundary in the four-tier hierarchy — closing this gap requires either removing the wildcard export (breaking change) or adding zone-level entry point assertions to sourcevision configuration (non-breaking).
- The rex CLI flat directory (src/cli/commands/) contains 30+ command files but only 3 are captured in named satellite zones — the other 27+ command files are zone-invisible, making the rex package's zone map unreliable as a structural navigation tool. Zone detection should be re-run with the full rex CLI directory in scope, or a catch-all 'rex-cli-commands' zone should be explicitly defined to surface the ungoverned CLI surface.
- sourcevision's static import graph analysis cannot detect dynamic imports (await import(), require() with variable paths, or import() in conditional branches). The confirmed 4 dynamic import sites in analyze.ts→chunked-review are invisible to zone coupling metrics. A supplementary CI check scanning for cross-zone dynamic imports would close this systematic enforcement blind spot before it grows.
- Audit the 12 entry points to determine which are prd-tree exports (which would move with reclassification) vs. genuine platform-level exports. If fewer than 4 entry points remain after prd-tree moves, the residual zone is a dissolution candidate rather than a consolidation target.
- Audit all 39 web-viewer → web-server imports to verify each passes through rex-gateway.ts or domain-gateway.ts. A single direct leaf import at this seam would be the highest-risk gateway violation in the monorepo given the 39-import volume. Add a CI assertion that no file outside src/viewer/rex-gateway.ts or src/viewer/domain-gateway.ts imports directly from src/server/.
- The web-viewer → web-server: 39 imports cross-zone edge is confirmed zero in the current codebase (web-viewer is a browser SPA that cannot import server modules at runtime). All governance priorities built around this being the highest-volume coupling edge are based on stale zone analysis data. Re-running sourcevision analysis to recalibrate the actual cross-zone edge map should be treated as a prerequisite for the next round of architectural governance decisions.
- Zone "Chunked Review" (chunked-review) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Add a build-freshness precondition note to tests/integration/README.md and to the root vitest config or package.json test script — e.g., a pretest or check-build step — to prevent integration tests from silently validating stale dist/ artifacts after source changes.
- Rename web-server-viewer-boundary.test.js to web-scheduler-gateway-boundary.test.js (or similar) to accurately reflect its coverage: scheduler boot wiring, gateway re-exports, and cleanup data flow. The current name implies import-direction enforcement for the 3 web-server→web-viewer reverse imports, which this file does not provide — a separate test or an addition to architecture-policy.test.js is needed to actually enforce that boundary.
- packages/rex/src/core/ hosts files from both prd-fix-command (core/fix.ts) and rex-core-utilities (keywords.ts, verify.ts) — two satellite zones sharing a physical directory is an ownership collision; inlining core/fix.ts into fix.ts (already the recommended structural fix) would also resolve the directory ambiguity and leave src/core/ exclusively owned by rex-core-utilities.
- rex-cli-e2e covers approximately 10% of the 30+ CLI commands (analyze, import, prune plus 1 adapter structural test) — the zone's perfect cohesion metrics give no indication of this coverage gap; add an explicit coverage inventory comment or README in the e2e directory listing which commands are intentionally deferred vs unintentionally uncovered.
- rex-core has zero internal imports between its 2 production files (keywords.ts, verify.ts) — these files form a zone only because community detection assigned them as cluster outliers, not because they share domain responsibility. Absorbing both files into rex-prd-engine would eliminate the zone, remove all 11 cross-zone edges associated with it, and resolve the bidirectional cycle without any logic changes.
- analyze.ts uses 4 dynamic `await import('./chunked-review.js')` call sites that create runtime coupling to the chunked-review satellite zone that is invisible to static import graph analysis. Static boundary enforcement tools (boundary-check.test.ts, domain-isolation.test.js) cannot detect dynamic imports. A test asserting that analyze.ts's dynamic import targets are declared dependencies rather than implicit runtime loads would close this enforcement blind spot.
- Define a named TypeScript interface (e.g., SchedulerCallbacks) for the injected broadcast, loadPRD, and collectAllIds parameters. Without an explicit interface, the callback contract is distributed across call sites in start.ts and parameter declarations in register-scheduler.ts — a refactor of either side can silently break the injection seam without triggering a type error.
- Add a hooks/index.ts barrel to packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ re-exporting all 29 hooks. Then add a boundary-check.test.ts assertion requiring consumers to import hooks through the barrel rather than by direct leaf path. This mirrors the crash/ and shared/ barrel enforcement pattern and creates a stable public contract for the hooks directory.
- Add web-dashboard-platform (33 files, cohesion 0.53) to the CLAUDE.md dual-fragility governance table with a 'watch' designation — it is 0.03 above the threshold, metrics are reliable at 33 files, and it has no named governance policy despite being larger than all three currently-listed dual-fragility zones. Documenting the current baseline cohesion (0.53) creates a reference point for evaluating future additions.
- Extend build-output-contract.test.ts to assert existence of key server bundle artifacts (e.g., dist/server/index.js or equivalent entry point). The current test covers both viewer and landing bundles but has a blind spot for server build output, meaning server build regressions are undetected until runtime.

#### Address pattern issues (11 findings)

```rex-meta
id: "5f81ff21-a931-49e0-8e76-2189bd06210f"
level: task
status: completed
priority: critical
completedAt: "2026-03-11T15:24:12.646Z"
resolutionDetail: Zone pins for batch-types.ts/keywords.ts/verify.ts, cli-dev.test.js timeout, boundary-check viewer-prd-interaction assertion, fix command e2e tests; remaining findings acknowledged as structural observations
resolutionType: code-change
source: sourcevision
startedAt: "2026-03-11T15:16:01.379Z"
_passthrough:
  recommendationMeta:
    findingHashes:
      - 8e7585ea7fe3
      - 1fc6497eb9e8
      - b4deeffda2c1
```
